definitions: The muscles of head and face include muscles of facial expression (innervated by Cranial Nerve VII) and muscles of mastication (innervated by Cranial Nerve V).
They are located in the superficial fascia and insert directly into the facial skin to regulate expression, eyelid close, chewing, and oral functions.
coreConcepts: Facial expression muscle motor coordination is controlled by the five branches of the Facial Nerve (Temporal, Zygomatic, Buccal, Marginal Mandibular, Cervical).
Masticatory muscles (Temporalis, Masseter, Medial Pterygoid, Lateral Pterygoid) originate from bones of skull and attach to the mandible to govern mandibular excursion.
equipment: Tuning fork CN VII sensory audit set

pearls: The buccinator muscle is pierced by the parotid gland salivation duct opposite the upper second molar tooth tooth segment.
precautions: Differentiate peripheral CN VII facial paralysis (Bell's Palsy - entire half face involved) from central stroke supranuclear palsy (spares upper forehead wrinkling).
indications: Diagnostics of Bell's Palsy facial weakness or trigeminal neuropathy
Evaluation of Temporomandibular Joint (TMJ) dysfunction muscle spasms

general: Deep myofascial needling during acute facial herpes zoster

treatmentProcedure: Audit CN VII by asking the student to screw eyes shut, puff cheeks, and elevate eyebrows.
Check CN V motor function by palpating both temporalis and masseter bulk during deep molar clenching.

mechanism: Masseter and Temporalis pull the mandible superiorly against the maxillary dental arch.
Pterygoids glide mandible accessory planes laterally to grind nutrients.

mcqs.question: Which branch of the cranial nerves innervates the muscles of facial expression?
mcqs.explanation: The Facial Nerve (CN VII) emerges from the stylo-mastoid foramen and branches inside the parotid gland to supply the motor components of facial expression.

viva.question: Name the nerve supplying the muscles of mastication. Mandibular Division of Trigeminal Nerve (CN V3).
Which facial muscle closes the lips and puckers the mouth? Orbicularis Oris.

references: Chaurasia B.D. Textbook of Anatomy - Vol 3 (Head, Neck & Brain)
Gray's Anatomy - Chapter 31 (Superficial Face Muscles and Scalp)

parameters.Orbicularis Oculi Role: Sphincter of eye; closes eyelids tightly (orbital part) or gently (palpebral part)
parameters.Buccinator Role: Compresses cheek against teeth; aids in blowing, sucking, and holding food
